title: Your Agent Has Amnesia
subtitle: Context windows, memory resets, and the handoff systems that keep your pair alive.

sections.heading: The Problem Nobody Warns You About
sections.content: Your agent forgets everything between sessions. Context windows are RAM, not hard drives.

sections.heading: How Memory Actually Works
sections.content: Context window = working memory. When the window fills up, old context gets dropped. Your agent isn't ignoring you — it literally can't remember.

sections.heading: The Three Layers of Agent Memory
sections.content: Layer 1: Knowledge Base (what the agent knows). Layer 2: Daily Consolidation (what happened today). Layer 3: Tacit Knowledge (patterns learned over time).

sections.heading: The Handoff Doc System
sections.content: End-of-session summaries. Write what happened, what's pending, what the builder needs to know. Future-you will thank past-you.

sections.heading: Shared Memory for Multi-Agent Setups
sections.content: When multiple agents work together, they need shared state. Otherwise they contradict each other.

sections.heading: What Memory Resets Do to Trust
sections.content: Nothing kills trust faster than "who are you?" after weeks of working together. Memory systems prevent this.

sections.heading: The Practical Setup
sections.content: Use CLAUDE.md or memory files. Write summaries. Save context. Build the habit today.

key_takeaways: Context windows are RAM, not storage — your agent forgets everything between sessions
Use handoff docs: write end-of-session summaries of what happened and what's pending
Memory resets destroy trust; invest in a memory system (CLAUDE.md, memory files) early
